question: What did we conclude was causing the WebSocket connections to drop silently?
turns: user
Users on our live collaboration feature reporting silent disconnections after periods of low activity. The app reconnects but they lose cursor positions. No errors in client logs.
user
Checked browser connection limits: Chrome allows 6 concurrent WebSocket connections per origin. Our app uses 2 at most. Not a browser concurrency limit issue.
user
Looked at SSL renegotiation as a possible cause. We use TLS 1.3 which has no renegotiation. Definitively ruled out.
user
Server-side heartbeat is implemented: we send a WebSocket ping frame every 30 seconds and expect a pong back. Client-side confirmed responding to pings. Heartbeat mechanism itself appears correct.
user
Measured exact drop times from server logs: disconnections cluster between 60 and 66 seconds after the last DATA frame (not ping/pong — actual application data). Never shorter than 60s.
user
That 60-second boundary is suspicious. Checked our AWS Application Load Balancer idle_timeout setting: it's set to 60 seconds.
user
AWS docs say the ALB idle timeout measures time since the last TCP segment in either direction. Ping/pong frames do travel as TCP segments so in theory they should reset it.
user

Ran controlled test: established WebSocket, sent only ping/pong every 30s, no application data. Connection dropped at exactly 60s. Ping frames are NOT resetting the ALB idle timer for WebSocket connections in our configuration.
user

Further testing confirmed the ALB closes the WebSocket connection after 60 seconds with no application-level data frame, regardless of ping/pong activity. This is a known ALB behavior with WebSockets — it specifically requires data frames, not just keepalive frames, to reset the idle timer.
user

Root cause: ALB idle timeout (60s) silently closes WebSocket connections that exchange only ping/pong frames but no application data, because the ALB does not count WebSocket ping/pong as activity for the idle timeout.
user

Fix options: increase ALB idle timeout for the WebSocket target group, or send a lightweight application data frame alongside the ping. Chose to increase ALB idle timeout to 300 seconds on the WebSocket target group.
user
Deployed. Tested idle connections over 6 hours. Zero silent disconnections. Collaboration feature stable.
user
Browser connection limits and TLS renegotiation definitively ruled out. Heartbeat was correct in intent but insufficient given ALB behavior.
